task_info: Write about a meaningful life event
task_full_text: Please describe a life event that still feels meaningful to you today.
writing_sample: When I moved away from home, I realized how much of my identity had depended on familiar people and places. At first I thought I was just lonely, but later I understood that the move forced me to decide which parts of myself were really mine and which came from habit.

task_info: Write about a meaningful life event
task_full_text: Please describe a life event that still feels meaningful to you today.
writing_sample: Failing an important exam embarrassed me more than I expected. Over time, though, I began to see that I had built too much of my self-worth around looking competent. The experience changed the standards I use to judge myself.

task_info: Compare current reflection to previous reflection
task_full_text: Read the participant's current text in relation to the previous one and judge whether it is deeper, more self-reflective, and more emotionally nuanced.
writing_sample: I now feel less angry about what happened and more curious about why it affected me so strongly. I can see that part of my reaction came from older insecurities, not just from the event itself.
comparison_sample_text: At the time I was mostly angry and thought the whole thing was unfair.

task_info: Write about someone you are grateful to
task_full_text: Please describe a person you feel grateful toward and explain why.
writing_sample: I used to think gratitude was just good manners, but writing about my grandfather made me notice how much of my patience I learned by watching him. He almost never lectured me. He just behaved in a way that made calmness look like strength.
